body:
Removed a reference to a paper on deploying machine learning components with OASIS3-MCT and Eophis.
They don't use Oceananigans; only cite it.
title: Remove reference to machine learning deployment paper

body:
Towards #4332.

This PR adds `NetCDFWriter` support for `OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid` and lays the plumbing for `MutableVerticalDiscretization` (z-star). Since `TripolarGrid`, `RotatedLatitudeLongitudeGrid`, and `ConformalCubedSpherePanelGrid` are all type aliases of `OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid`, supporting OSSG covers all three.

cc @simone-silvestri @vopikamm — would value your eyes on this, especially:
- the staggered-name conventions for the 2D `λ`/`φ` aux coords,
- the `r` vs `z` naming choice for the reference vertical coord on MVD grids,
- the best-effort `constructor_arguments` for `RotatedLatitudeLongitudeGrid`.

## Approach: CF §5.2 auxiliary coordinates

For OSSG, this PR writes:

- **Bare logical-index dimensions** `i_caa`/`i_faa`/`j_aca`/`j_afa` (created by `defDim` only; no coordinate variable on them — matches MOM6/NEMO conventions).
- **Eight 2D auxiliary coordinate variables** `λ_cca`/`λ_fca`/`λ_cfa`/`λ_ffa` and `φ_*` at every Arakawa-C stagger location, each dimensioned `(i_*, j_*)`.
- **Per-field `coordinates` attribute**: every data field gets `coordinates = "λ_** φ_** z_aac"` so xarray, ncview, Panoply, CDO etc. pick up the right lat/lon pair.

Example tracer field on a TripolarGrid:

```
T(i_caa, j_aca, z_aac, time) ;
  T:coordinates = "λ_cca φ_cca z_aac" ;
```

## MVD: `r` for the reference coord

For `MutableVerticalDiscretization` (z-star), the on-disk 1D vertical coordinate is `r_aac`/`r_aaf` (Lagrangian reference). The physical `z = z(r, η, …)` is reconstructible from `r` and the time-varying free surface `η` at read time.

`StaticVerticalDiscretization` is unaffected — it keeps writing `z_aac`/`z_aaf` exactly as before.

Plumbed through a tiny new helper:

```julia
vertical_coordinate_name(::StaticVerticalDiscretization)  = "z"
vertical_coordinate_name(::MutableVerticalDiscretization) = "r"
```

…called at every site in `dimensions.jl` that previously hardcoded `"z"`.

## Architectural changes (touch all grids, all gated to be no-ops for existing grids)

1. **`create_spatial_dimensions!` accepts `(array, dims)` namedtuples.** This is what unlocks 2D aux coords sitting on bare 1D dimensions. The 1D coord-variable case (Rectilinear, LatitudeLongitudeGrid, all vertical) is auto-wrapped via a `nc_variable` helper and behaves identically.
2. **`create_field_dimensions!` short-circuits if dims already exist.** During the normal `initialize_nc_file` flow `gather_dimensions` has already created everything, so we just look up names instead of trying (and failing) to re-zip 2D `λ`/`φ` arrays against bare 1D dim names.

## What's not in this PR (will iterate)

- Time-varying η output for MVD (the metric lifecycle — needs `write_output!` extension)
- OSSG grid metrics (`Δx_**`, `Δy_**`, `Az_**`) — only the eight coord aux vars are written so far
- FieldTimeSeries round-trip tests for OSSG
- Tests in `test/test_netcdf_writer.jl` covering the new grids
- Formal CF `formula_terms` on `r_aac` for z-star

These will land as follow-up commits on this branch before merge.

## Smoke validation (local)

- TripolarGrid write produces correct dim names and `coordinates` attributes.
- Non-rotated `RotatedLatitudeLongitudeGrid` produces field values **identical** to the equivalent `LatitudeLongitudeGrid` and coord arrays matching to roundoff — the cross-validation linchpin from the design plan.
- Rectilinear and LatLon regression: no behavior change in file structure.

The cross-validation result:

```
LLG  λ_caa: [11.25, 33.75, 56.25, 78.75, 101.25]
RLLG λ_cca[:,1]: [11.249999999999998, 33.75, 56.25, 78.75, 101.25]
LLG  T[8,6,2,end]: 0.6846098
RLLG T[8,6,2,end]: 0.6846098
```

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Support OrthogonalSphericalShellGrid in NetCDFWriter (CF §5.2 + MutableVerticalDiscretization plumbing)

body:
At the moment we are missing a `set!` function to support loading chunked memory from split file paths. This PR introduces it.  MWE (which works on this branch and fails on main):
```julia
using Oceananigans
using Oceananigans.OutputReaders: SplitFilePath

grid = RectilinearGrid(size=(2, 2, 2), extent=(1, 1, 1))
model = HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel(grid; tracers=:c, buoyancy=nothing)

set_tracer_to_iteration(sim) = fill!(parent(sim.model.tracers.c), sim.model.clock.iteration)
simulation = Simulation(model, Δt=1, stop_iteration=10, verbose=false)
add_callback!(simulation, set_tracer_to_iteration, IterationInterval(1))

simulation.output_writers[:c] = JLD2Writer(model, model.tracers;
                                           filename = "split_mwe",
                                           schedule = IterationInterval(1),
                                           file_splitting = TimeInterval(3),
                                           overwrite_existing = true)
run!(simulation)
full   = FieldTimeSeries("split_mwe.jld2", "c")
partly = FieldTimeSeries("split_mwe.jld2", "c"; backend=InMemory(2))

for n in 1:length(partly.times)
    a = interior(full[n])[1, 1, 1]
    b = interior(partly[n])[1, 1, 1]
    @test a == b
end
```
on main returns:
```julia
┌ Warning: No data found for time 2.0e+00 and time index 3
│ for field /Users/simonesilvestri/development/Oceananigans.jl/split_mwe_part4.jld2 at path c
└ @ Oceananigans.OutputReaders ~/development/Oceananigans.jl/src/OutputReaders/set_field_time_series.jl:67
┌ Warning: No data found for time 3.0e+00 and time index 4
│ for field /Users/simonesilvestri/development/Oceananigans.jl/split_mwe_part4.jld2 at path c
└ @ Oceananigans.OutputReaders ~/development/Oceananigans.jl/src/OutputReaders/set_field_time_series.jl:67
Test Failed at REPL[14]:4
  Expression: a == b
   Evaluated: 2.0 == 0.0

ERROR: There was an error during testing
```
title: Support `InMemory(N)` with split timeseries

body:
## Summary

Three issues blocked the `LagrangianAveragedDynamicSmagorinsky` regression test from running correctly:

1. **Duplicate `for` loop** in `test_nonhydrostatic_regression.jl` — outer loop bindings were shadowed by an inner loop with identical content (each closure tested 16× instead of 4×). Collapsed to one loop.

2. **State restored via direct `.=` instead of `set!`** in `ocean_large_eddy_simulation_regression_test.jl` — bypassed `initialize_closure_fields!`, leaving `𝒥ᴹᴹ = 0` and permanently disabling the Lagrangian closure. Now uses `set!(...; enforce_incompressibility=false)` and explicitly restores `closure_fields` from the checkpoint.

3. **`previous_compute_time::Ref` not restored** — the closure-restoration loop only handled `Field` leaves, leaving the `Ref` at the model's initial `clock.time = 0`. The first time step computed `Δt_lagrangian = clock.time - 0 = 20000` instead of `Δt = 2`, which drove `ϵ ≈ 1` in `_lagrangian_average_LM_MM!` and effectively discarded the carefully-restored 𝒥 history. The loop now copies `Base.RefValue` leaves alongside `Field` leaves.

Plus supporting loader changes: auto-detect new (`simulation/model/...`) vs legacy checkpoint paths, and a `load_interior(data, target_size)` helper that auto-detects how many halo layers are saved and unwraps `OffsetArray`s.

With all three in place, the regression matches the reference at iteration 10010 to machine epsilon (~1e-15) across all four closures (AMD, SmagorinskyLilly, DirectionalDynamicSmagorinsky, LagrangianDynamicSmagorinsky).

## Notes

CI will fail on `LagrangianDynamicSmagorinsky` until the companion artifacts PR is merged — the previously-committed reference data was incompatible with the fixed closure code.

**Companion artifacts PR**: glwagner/OceananigansArtifacts.jl#7

## Test plan

- [x] `nonhydrostatic_regression` group passes locally with all four closures (Δ at machine epsilon)
- [x] CI passes after glwagner/OceananigansArtifacts.jl#7 is merged

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Fix broken LagrangianAveraged DynamicSmagorinsky regression tests

body:
## Summary

`PerturbationAdvection` previously had no custom display method, so it fell back to Julia's default struct printing:

```julia
PerturbationAdvection{Float64, Nothing}(1.0, 10.0, 0.0, nothing)
```

This is hard to read — the four positional values give no hint of which field is which.

This PR adds `Base.summary` and a tree-style `Base.show`, modeled on the existing `RiBasedVerticalDiffusivity` show method:

```julia
PerturbationAdvection{Float64}
├── inflow_timescale: 1.0
├── outflow_timescale: 10.0
├── gravity_wave_speed: 0.0
└── density: Nothing
```

## Changes

- `src/BoundaryConditions/perturbation_advection.jl`: add `Base.summary` and `Base.show`, plus an explicit `using Oceananigans.Utils: prettysummary` import.
- `docs/src/models/boundary_conditions.md`: update the existing `jldoctest` output to match the new display.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Add `show` method for `PerturbationAdvection`

body:
#5586 routed `set_to_field!` through `on_architecture(child_architecture(u), v)`
+ `fill_halo_regions!(v_on_u)` + `interpolate!` whenever `matching_field_discretization` is false. On distributed fields that path is broken in two ways:

1. `indices(u) == indices(v)` rejects equivalent windows like `(:, :, :)` vs `(1:Nx, 1:Ny, 1:Nz)`, sending well-formed `set!` calls down the else branch unnecessarily. `TransposableField` constructs zfield/yfield/xfield with explicit unit-range indices, so `set!(Φ.zfield, ϕ)` in `test_distributed_transpose.jl` always falls through.

2. `on_architecture(child_architecture(u), v)` strips `Distributed{CPU}` to `CPU` on the grid but leaves the `DistributedCommunicationBoundaryCondition`s (and the precompiled `DistributedFillHalo` kernels) intact. The subsequent `fill_halo_regions!(v_on_u)` then dispatches to the non-distributed path and invokes `DistributedFillHalo{WestAndEast}` with 6 args via the inlined `fill_halo_event!`, when the only method takes 7 (`c, bc1, bc2, loc, grid, arch, buffers`) — MethodError.

Fixes:

* `matching_field_discretization` now treats `Colon` and the equivalent full `UnitRange` as the same window (per-dimension, parameterized by size). Same size + same location + equivalent windows now correctly takes the cheap copy path.

* `set_to_field!` fills halos on `v`'s native architecture before migrating, so distributed dispatch is used when `v` is distributed. The post-migration `interpolate!` then samples a field whose halos are already populated.

Repros via `test/test_distributed_transpose.jl` (passed at parent of #5586, failed at the merge of #5586).
title: Fix `set!(::DistributedField, ::Field)` regression from #5586
